canonical_definition: Based on the available data, ToolACE is a dataset used for evaluation in the area of function-calling safety. The single source paper defines it as a collection of 11,300 rows intended to validate the generalization capability of models on these tasks. It is explicitly framed not as a standalone benchmark but as a supplementary or complementary evaluation set. For instance, one study utilizes it alongside MCIP-Bench to "further validate the generalization capability" ("TheLLMAlready Knows: EstimatingLLM-Perceived Question Difficulty via Hidden Representations"). Therefore, its documented use is as an additional resource for assessing model performance in safety-related function-calling scenarios.
papers: TheLLMAlready Knows: EstimatingLLM-Perceived Question Difficulty via Hidden Representations
Towards a Holistic and Automated Evaluation Framework for Multi-Level Comprehension ofLLMs in Book-Length Contexts
Translation in the Hands of Many: Centering Lay Users in Machine Translation Interactions

definitions.text: A dataset containing 11,300 rows used as a supplementary evaluation set to validate generalization capability in function-calling safety tasks.
definitions.paper: TheLLMAlready Knows: EstimatingLLM-Perceived Question Difficulty via Hidden Representations
